name: tavern-common
summary: The common room of a working tavern. Bar along one wall, mixed tables, hearth, stairs up to rooms. Smoky, noisy, central to a town's social life.

accent.purpose: worn footpath from door to bar

primary_palette.note: bar-forward tavern, barkeep at the center
fireplace-forward — quieter tavern where regulars claim the hearth
entertainment-forward — bard's tavern, stage is the focus

story_prompts: busy evening — every table full, bard playing, barkeep swamped
mid-afternoon lull — one patron at the bar, barkeep wiping glasses
post-brawl — overturned chairs, blood-smear on a table, regulars gossiping
closing time — last patrons staggering out, barkeep sweeping
anti_patterns: don't grid-align tables — organic, uneven placement reads as 'busy space'
the bar needs a back-bar / bottle shelf — a counter alone looks wrong
leave clear paths from door → bar and door → stairs — patrons need to move
